warning: AI-assisted monitoring. Verify against official legislation before product approval, export, label declaration, or customer compliance confirmation.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Additives DB (GSFA Online)
rules.notes: The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets conditions for permitted food additive use in all foods. Users should consult the Preamble for interpretation. The database is regularly updated.
rules.evidenceQuote: The "Codex General Standard for Food Additives" (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods

rules.limit: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and CCCF established levels
rules.sourceTitle: Contaminants
rules.notes: Codex establishes maximum levels (MLs) for contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants to ensure food safety. These levels are legally permitted maximum concentrations. The Codex Committee on Contaminants in Food (CCCF) establishes and endorses these levels.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Codex maximum level (ML) for a contaminant in a food or feed commodity is the maximum concentration of that substance recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission to be legally permitted in that commodity.

rules.limit: Verification required via CXC 78-2017 Code of Practice for the Prevention and Reduction of Mycotoxins in Spices
rules.sourceTitle: Contaminants
rules.notes: A specific Code of Practice exists for the prevention and reduction of mycotoxins in spices, providing guidance to minimize contamination.
rules.evidenceQuote: CXC 78-2017 Code of Practice for the Prevention and Reduction of Mycotoxins in Spices

rules.parameter: Additives (excluding natural flavoring agents and generally consumed substances used as additives)
rules.limit: Prohibited unless specified as unlikely to cause harm by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act (Act No. 233 of 1947, Last Version: Act No. 46 of 2018)
rules.notes: This establishes a positive list principle for additives.
rules.evidenceQuote: Additives (excluding natural flavoring agents and things that have generally been served for human consumption, and are used as additives) and preparations and food containing additives must not be sold... except for cases that the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies as unlikely to cause harm to human health

rules.parameter: Methods of producing, processing, using, cooking, or preserving food or additives; Ingredients of food or additives
rules.limit: Must conform to criteria/standards established by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare. Verification required.
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act (Act No. 233 of 1947, Last Version: Act No. 46 of 2018)
rules.notes: The specific criteria and standards are established by MHLW and must be checked.
rules.evidenceQuote: the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare may establish the criteria for the methods of producing, processing, using, cooking, or preserving food or additives... or may establish standards for the ingredients of food or additives... food or additives must not be produced, processed, used, cooked, or preserved using methods that do not conform to such criteria; food or additives that do not conform to such criteria must not be sold or imported; and food or additives that do not conform to such standards must not be produced, imported, processed, used, cooked, preserved, or sold.

rules.limit: Prohibited if likely to cause harm due to contamination or addition of foreign substances
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act (Act No. 233 of 1947, Last Version: Act No. 46 of 2018)
rules.notes: This is a general prohibition against harmful contamination.
rules.evidenceQuote: those which are likely to cause harm to human health due to contamination or the addition of foreign substances or on other grounds

rules.parameter: Residues of agricultural chemicals, feed additives, or animal pharmaceuticals
rules.limit: Must not exceed quantities specified by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare as unlikely to cause harm. Verification required.
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act (Act No. 233 of 1947, Last Version: Act No. 46 of 2018)
rules.notes: This refers to the 'Positive List System' for residues. Specific limits are set by MHLW.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food in which substances that are the ingredients of agricultural chemicals... and pharmaceutical products... remain in a quantity exceeding the quantity that the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies as unlikely to cause harm to human health... must not be produced, imported, processed, used, cooked, preserved, for the purpose of sale, or sold

rules.limit: Prohibited, unless specified as harmless by MHLW
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act (Act No. 233 of 1947, Last Version: Act No. 46 of 2018)
rules.notes: This applies to substances contained in or borne by food or additives.
rules.evidenceQuote: those which contain or bear toxic or harmful substances or are suspected to contain or bear such substances; provided, however, that this does not apply when the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies that the substances are unlikely to cause harm to human health

rules.parameter: Food business operator responsibility for safety and information
rules.limit: Must endeavor to ensure safety, obtain knowledge, ensure raw material safety, conduct voluntary inspections, record necessary information (e.g., seller name), and take prompt measures for hazards.
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act (Act No. 233 of 1947, Last Version: Act No. 46 of 2018)
rules.notes: General responsibility for food business operators.
rules.evidenceQuote: A food business operator... on their own responsibility, must endeavor to ensure the safety of the food, additives... and for that purpose, they must endeavor to obtain the knowledge and skills necessary... ensure the safety of raw materials... conduct voluntary inspections... make a record of any necessary information... and retain the record

rules.parameter: Handling of food or additives for sale
rules.limit: Must be collected, produced, processed, used, cooked, stored, transported, displayed, and delivered in a clean and sanitary manner.
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act (Act No. 233 of 1947, Last Version: Act No. 46 of 2018)
rules.notes: General hygiene requirement for all stages of handling food and additives.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food or additives which are meant to be sold... must be collected, produced, processed, used, cooked, stored, transported, displayed and delivered in a clean and sanitary manner.

rules.limit: Prohibited if contaminated with pathogens and likely to cause harm
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act (Act No. 233 of 1947, Last Version: Act No. 46 of 2018)
rules.notes: This applies to food or additives contaminated with pathogens.
rules.evidenceQuote: those which are contaminated with pathogens or are suspected to be so, and which are likely to cause harm to human health

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.parameter: Class II chemical preservatives (e.g., sorbic acid, benzoic acid)
rules.limit: Calculation tool must be used for permitted levels if multiple are present
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: If two or more Class II chemical preservatives are used, the Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives must be used.
rules.evidenceQuote: If two or more Class II chemical preservatives are to be used in the food (e.g. sorbic acid or benzoic acid), use the Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives to calculate the permitted levels that should be used.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: All food businesses offering food for sale in Singapore must comply.
rules.evidenceQuote: All food businesses offering food for sale in Singapore must comply with the regulatory limits on food additives.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Different limits apply depending on the additive and the type of food products. Refer to the Guidance Information on Requirements for Use of Food Additives and SFA's Legislation.
rules.evidenceQuote: Different limits apply, depending on the additive and the type of food products. Use the Guidance Information on Requirements for Use of Food Additives to learn more about the different types of additives and the corresponding regulations that impose the limits.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.limit: Must be assessed for safety by SFA
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Only food additives that have been assessed by SFA are permitted for use in food products sold in Singapore.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives must be assessed for safety by SFA before they can be used in food sold in Singapore.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Food additives are allowed when there is a technological justification and they do not present health risks to consumers. Use the Food Additives Search tool or download the List of Permitted Food Additives.
rules.evidenceQuote: Download the List of Permitted Food Additives or use the Food Additives Search tool to check if your additives are allowed.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.limit: Mandatory awareness and testing as needed
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Food businesses must be aware of commonly found contaminants in their products and conduct laboratory tests as needed. Can check with suppliers or use SFA's Laboratory Recognition Programme (LRP) laboratories.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food businesses must be aware of the contaminants that are commonly found in their food products and conduct laboratory tests as needed.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.parameter: Compliance with regulatory limits for contaminants
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: All food businesses must comply with the regulatory limits on incidental constituents (contaminants) in food.
rules.evidenceQuote: All food businesses must comply with the regulatory limits on incidental constituents i.e. contaminants in food.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.limit: Must be within SFA's regulatory limits
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Contaminants are substances present in food that can occur during production, manufacturing, treatment, packing, packaging, transport, or holding, or as a result of environmental contamination.
rules.evidenceQuote: Contaminants (also known as incidental constituents) in food sold in Singapore must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: If laboratory test results indicate contaminants exceed regulatory limits, the food product must not be imported or sold.
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food or food products contain contaminants or incidental constituents that exceed the regulatory limits, do not import or sell them.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: SFA has established a list of maximum residue limits (MRLs) for other veterinary drug residues, effective from 1 May 2020. These MRLs can be downloaded from the SFA website.
rules.evidenceQuote: SFA has also assessed the risk of the presence of other veterinary drug residues in various food commodities, and has established a list of maximum residue limits (MRLs) for veterinary drug residues for compliance by food businesses with effect from 1 May 2020. These MRLs can be downloaded from the SFA website at the following weblink.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.parameter: Specific contaminant limits (Microbiological, Heavy metals, Mycotoxins, Marine biotoxins, Veterinary drug residues, Pesticide residues, Other incidental constituents)
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Various limits apply to different types of contaminants. Refer to the relevant SFA documents for details.
rules.evidenceQuote: There are various limits imposed on different types of contaminants or incidental constituents in food. For more details, please refer to the relevant documents below: Microbiological standards, Heavy metals, Mycotoxins and Marine biotoxins, Veterinary drug residues, Pesticide residues, Other incidental constituents.

rules.foodCategory: food derived from meat (as an ingredient in seasoning/snacks)
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Prohibition applies to the import or sale of meat and any food derived from meat containing these specific compounds.
rules.evidenceQuote: Regulation 33 of the Food Regulations prohibits the import or sale of meat and any food derived from meat which contains residues of the following compounds: diethylstibestrol (3, 4-bis(p-hydroxyphenyl)-3-hexene) hexoestrol (3, 4-bis(p-hydroxyphenyl)-n-hexane) dienoestrol (3, 4-bis(p-hydroxyphenyl)-2, 4-hexadiene)

rules.foodCategory: prepacked seasoning powder, prepacked seasoning sauce, prepacked snack seasoning, prepacked ready-to-eat snacks
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Applies only to prepacked foods for sale in Singapore.
rules.evidenceQuote: All ingredients and additives used in food products must be declared in list of ingredients on food labels.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Ready-to-eat (RTE) food are not required to be tested for total plate count (TPC).
rules.evidenceQuote: Ready-to-eat (RTE) food are not required to be tested for total plate count (TPC).

rules.sourceTitle: Food Additive Listings | FDA
rules.notes: Food additives and color additives must be listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173 and Parts 73, 74 respectively) or be Generally Recognized As Safe (GRAS) or prior-sanctioned.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives and color additives that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173 and Parts 73, 74 respectively)

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: GRAS substances must be listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 182 and 184) or be subject to a GRAS Notice.
rules.evidenceQuote: Generally Recognized as Safe ("GRAS") substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 182 and 184).

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: Substances listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) as prohibited from use in food are not allowed.
rules.evidenceQuote: Prohibited substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) as prohibited from use in food

rules.limit: Milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: These 9 allergens are recognized by U.S. law (FALCPA and FASTER Act).
rules.evidenceQuote: The major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame.

rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool
rules.notes: This is a Guidance Level.
rules.evidenceQuote: 3-Monochloropropane-1,2-Diol (3-MCPD) Acid-hydrolyzed protein and Asian-style sauces Guidance Level 1 ppm

rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool
rules.notes: This is an Action Level.
rules.evidenceQuote: Aflatoxins, total Foods Action Level 20 ppb

rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool
rules.notes: This is an Action Level.
rules.evidenceQuote: Aflatoxins, total Peanuts and peanut products Action Level 20 ppb

rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool
rules.notes: This is an Action Level.

rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool
rules.notes: Contaminant levels (tolerances, action levels, guidance levels, etc.) for poisonous or deleterious substances in human food are published in various guidance documents and regulations. Tolerances and action levels are defined in 21 CFR parts 109 and 509. The Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool provides a consolidated list.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA has published a variety of contaminant levels for poisonous or deleterious substances in human food in various guidance documents and regulations for industry.

rules.limit: Not required by law and should not be used as a substitute for adhering to current good manufacturing practices
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Such statements must be truthful and not misleading and are used to address unavoidable cross-contact after all precautions have been taken.
rules.evidenceQuote: Such statements are not required by law and can be used to address unavoidable "cross-contact," only if manufacturers have incorporated good manufacturing processes in their facility and have taken every precaution to avoid cross-contact

rules.limit: Facilities must implement allergen preventive controls and written procedures
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: The CGMP & PC rule (21 CFR part 117) establishes requirements for allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact and ensure accurate labeling.
rules.evidenceQuote: The CGMP & PC rule includes requirements for allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact in manufacturing and packaging and to prevent undeclared allergens.

rules.limit: Must be declared on the label
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Identified as allergenic substances derived from insects.
rules.evidenceQuote: Color additives made from cochineal extract and carmine... have been identified as allergenic substances that must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.

rules.limit: Must appear in parentheses following the name of the ingredient
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: This requirement is met if the common or usual name of an ingredient already identifies that allergen's food source name (e.g., buttermilk).
rules.evidenceQuote: The name of the food source of a major food allergen must appear: In parentheses following the name of the ingredient.

rules.limit: Must appear immediately after or next to the list of ingredients in a 'contains' statement
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Example: 'Contains wheat, milk, and soy.'
rules.evidenceQuote: Immediately after or next to the list of ingredients in a 'contains' statement.

rules.parameter: Declaration of specific tree nut, fish, and Crustacean shellfish
rules.limit: Type of tree nut, species of fish, and species of Crustacean shellfish must be declared
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Examples: almonds, pecans, walnuts for tree nuts; bass, flounder, cod for fish; crab, lobster, shrimp for Crustacean shellfish.
rules.evidenceQuote: The law also requires that the type of tree nut (for example, almonds, pecans, walnuts), and the species of fish (for example, bass, flounder, cod) and Crustacean shellfish (for example, crab, lobster, shrimp) to be declared.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Required due to potential hypersensitivity reactions in some individuals.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA requires all products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 to identify it on their labels

rules.limit: Must meet defined standard for gluten content
rules.effectiveDate: August 2, 2013 (final rule defining 'gluten-free'); August 12, 2020 (compliance for fermented/hydrolyzed foods)
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: A final rule defines 'gluten-free' for food labeling. Compliance requirements also exist for fermented and hydrolyzed foods bearing the 'gluten-free' claim.
rules.evidenceQuote: On August 2, 2013, the FDA issued a final rule defining "gluten-free" for food labeling... On August 12, 2020, the FDA issued a final rule to establish compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods... bearing the "gluten-free" claim.

rules.limit: Must be declared when concentration is ≥10 total sulfites
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Required due to adverse reactions, such as asthma, in sensitive individuals.
rules.evidenceQuote: Various sulfiting agents... must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration in the food is ≥10 parts per million total sulfites.

rules.sourceTitle: Nghị định 37/2026/NĐ-CP (referenced by Thông tư 24/2026/TT-BKHCN)
rules.notes: This rule refers to Decree No. 37/2026/ND-CP (dated 23/01/2026), which details the implementation of the Law on Product and Goods Quality. Food products, including seasoning and snack products, must comply with the quality standards and measures outlined in these legal instruments. The specific quality parameters and limits need to be verified from Decree 37/2026/NĐ-CP and the underlying Law.
rules.evidenceQuote: Nghị định số 37/2026/NĐ-CP ngày 23 tháng 01 năm 2026 của Chính phủ quy định chi tiết một số điều và biện pháp để tổ chức, hướng dẫn thi hành Luật Chất lượng sản phẩm, hàng hóa

rules.sourceTitle: Thông tư 28/2026/TT-BCT
rules.notes: This regulation establishes a list of imported goods (identified by HS codes) that are subject to state inspection for food safety under the management of the Ministry of Industry and Trade. Specific food safety parameters and limits for these goods, including for seasoning and snack products, would be detailed in the accompanying list or other relevant food safety regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: Ban hành Danh mục các mặt hàng nhập khẩu (kèm theo mã số HS) thực hiện kiểm tra nhà nước về an toàn thực phẩm thuộc trách nhiệm quản lý nhà nước của Bộ Công Thương

rules.parameter: Specialized inspection procedures in health sector
rules.sourceTitle: Thông tư 17/2026/TT-BYT
rules.notes: This regulation specifies the specialized inspection procedures in the health sector. Food products, including seasoning and snack products, must comply with these procedures when subject to health inspections.
rules.evidenceQuote: Quy định về quy trình kiểm tra chuyên ngành lĩnh vực y tế
